mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and reduce the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold, or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer and capital demand channels, supporting higher corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series decouples, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher short-term equity valuations as markets price in improved operational efficiency and earnings capacity.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Breaching the 202 inflation index threshold signals persistent price pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are non-negative in >60% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 202, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a robust labor market that sustains household income and consumption, driving positive corporate earnings revisions and supporting S&P 500 appreciation over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or predictive power decays to Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized acceleration in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, reducing recession probabilities and triggering positive short-term equity momentum as investors reprice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both grow > 1.5% quarter-over-quarter
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient becomes insignificant in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production gains signal expanding supply capacity that meets rising demand, supporting stable margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over one month when the economy operates near full capacity.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive returns when capacity utilization exceeds 85%, or the signal flips negative during high-utilization regimes.
